finding: TEST-17's 'shared module via cross-workspace cassette' test targets slug settings-users, but that page is populated by User.list, which is present in the desktop crawl base (fixtures/crawl.generated.ts). The web user/gallery.tsx contributes only `overlays` (no `cassette`), so SHARED_CASSETTE seeds NOTHING for that page. The assertion (>40 chars, no errors) passes identically from the crawl base alone, even if module-seed.ts's `...SHARED_CASSETTE` spread were deleted or the cross-workspace `@/dev/gallery/support/registry` import were broken. The test therefore does not verify the cross-workspace cassette inheritance it claims to prove. A faithful target is a shared surface whose data comes ONLY from a web module `cassette` key absent from crawl (e.g. scheduler ScheduledTask.list, notification Notification.unreadCount, knowledge-base, voice).

module-seed.ts exports OVERLAY_ENTRIES (SHARED_OVERLAYS + desktop overlays) but nothing consumes it: fixtures/index.ts imports only MODULE_CASSETTE, and desktop overlays.tsx defines its OWN hardcoded `OVERLAY_ENTRIES = []` with a DIFFERENT OverlayEntry shape (render/label vs the web component/title/open). The web overlays.tsx re-exports registry's OVERLAY_ENTRIES and pages/overlays render them; the desktop gallery does not. So the desktop gallery inherits ONLY the shared cassette, never shared web overlay/seeded/deepState surfaces — the 'inherits the web seed for free' claim is cassette-only. Dead export; also a parity gap if overlay coverage on desktop is intended (desktop overlays.tsx documents a deliberate page-focus, so likely intended, but the export is misleading).

Slug uniqueness is enforced per-set only: assertUniqueSlugs(DESKTOP_GALLERIES) checks the desktop-only set; the web registry checks its own DISCOVERED set separately. There is NO cross-set (shared-web vs desktop-only) slug-collision guard, so a desktop-only overlay/deep/seeded slug that duplicates a shared web slug would NOT be caught, silently producing duplicate slugs in the merged OVERLAY_ENTRIES — violating registry-core's stated contract ('Slugs must be unique across overlays/deep/seeded'). Current blast radius is low because desktop does not enumerate seeded/overlay/deep surfaces (only the cassette), but it is a latent invariant break the moment desktop wires those surfaces.

SHARED_CASSETTE (web-typed Cassette, keyed by the WEB ApiEndpoint union + web response types) is spread into the desktop Cassette via `as Cassette`. The cast suppresses any compile-time check that a shared endpoint's response SHAPE matches the desktop api-client type. Practically safe: shared surfaces render the same web components (via the @/ fallback) against web-shaped data, and a web-only endpoint key is simply never matched by the desktop mockApi (harmless extra key). No runtime defect found, but there is no guard against a genuine web-vs-desktop endpoint divergence.

Cross-workspace resolution is sound. `@/dev/gallery/support/registry` has no desktop shadow, so both tsc (paths `@/*` -> ../../ui/src/*) and vite (localOverridePlugin Tier1 desktop miss -> Tier3 core) resolve to the WEB registry — consistent. import.meta.glob is transformed per physical file location: the web registry globs `../../../modules` = ui/src/modules (shared seed); desktop module-seed globs `../../modules` = desktop/ui/src/modules (desktop-only). Different dirs, no double-glob, no resolution cycle. Verified via tsconfig.json + vite-plugin-local-override.ts.

The memory NO-SEED allow-list rationale is correct. Adding desktop/ui/src/modules/memory/gallery.tsx WOULD be a raw whole-file shadow of the existing ui/src/modules/memory/gallery.tsx and be flagged by gen-override-registry.mjs's raw-shadow gate (the modules tree is scanned; only module.tsx is a recorded SHADOW-EXCEPTION). And the memory-combined page's mount endpoints (Memory.list, MemorySettings.get, MemoryAdmin.get) are all present in the desktop crawl base, so it renders populated. Dev-only surface; no security-relevant override logic dropped.

The shared gate invocation is correct + portable: `node ../../ui/scripts/gen-gallery-seed-registry.mjs --src src --check`. `--src` is a positional arg (no Windows-unsafe inline env prefix); path.resolve('src') against the desktop CWD -> desktop/ui/src; MODULES_DIR/OUT/EXCEPTIONS_PATH all derive from SRC and point at the desktop tree. B6-safe (reads only committed module.tsx / gallery.tsx / exceptions.md, never .lifecycle/). Gate runs clean (9 modules, 4 seeded, 1 allow-listed). The gallery.tsx exclusion is applied to BOTH desktop gate copies (gen-gallery-coverage.mjs excludes basename gallery.tsx; gen-state-matrix.mjs excludes gallery.tsx/gallery.ts).
